title: WOS Verification Report
description: A WOS Verification Report sidecar document. Records the results of SMT-based formal verification of deontic constraints and governance rules. Captures which constraints were verified, the result (proven-safe, proven-unsafe, inconclusive), counterexamples, and solver metadata. Reports are immutable provenance artifacts.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)

$wosVerificationReport.description: WOS Verification Report specification version. MUST be '1.0'.

x-lm.intent: Document type marker for WOS Verification Report sidecar

targetWorkflow.description: URI of the WOS Kernel Document whose constraints were verified. Follows the same targetWorkflow binding pattern as Layer 1 and Layer 2 sidecars.

x-lm.intent: Binds this report to the verified workflow

examples.notes: Constraint holds for all possible input combinations

x-lm.intent: The verification outcome for each constraint -- proven-safe, proven-unsafe, or inconclusive

constraintRef.description: Reference to the deontic constraint that was verified.

x-lm.intent: Identifies which constraint this result applies to

result.description: Verification result. 'proven-safe': constraint holds for all possible inputs within declared domains. 'proven-unsafe': a counterexample exists. 'inconclusive': solver timed out or construct outside verifiable subset.

x-lm.intent: The formal verification outcome for this constraint

solverTimeMs.description: Time the solver spent on this constraint in milliseconds.

notes.description: Additional notes from the verification process.

inputs.description: Input values that violate the constraint.

explanation.description: Human-readable explanation of why this input violates the constraint.

ExtensionsMap.description: Vendor extension data attached to this node. All keys MUST start with 'x-' (see Kernel §10.6). The reserved namespace 'x-wos-*' is for WOS Working Group use only; third-party extensions MUST use a unique vendor prefix (e.g., 'x-acme-', 'x-vendor-'). Processors MUST ignore unknown extension keys to preserve forward compatibility. Extension values are unconstrained — any JSON value is valid, but authors are encouraged to document the shape in their vendor spec.

JsonSchemaUri.description: Optional JSON Schema URI enabling editor validation and autocompletion. When present, editors (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.) validate the document against this schema. Omit in production documents; the authoritative schema URI is derived from the document type marker (e.g., '$wosKernel': '1.0'). Used for author-time tooling only — runtime processors MUST ignore this field.
